title: The Anarchist Cookbook
description: A notorious manual of sabotage, explosives, and anti-state provocation.
descriptionParagraphs: A notorious manual of sabotage, explosives, and anti-state provocation.

The Anarchist Cookbook is organized less as a story than as an argument. As a instructional manual, it tries to persuade readers through selection, emphasis, and direct claims about violence, instruction, and anti state.

Its significance lies in the way it compresses large claims into memorable formulas and positions. Even readers who reject the work usually have to reckon with how sharply it frames questions about violence, instruction, and anti state.

cover.alt: The Anarchist Cookbook cover thumbnail from Wikipedia

overview.englishDescription: A notorious manual of sabotage, explosives, and anti-state provocation.
banningParagraphs: The Anarchist Cookbook entered censorship debates as a instructional manual associated with violence, instruction, and anti state. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around violence and instructional harm.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1970s onward in Australia, where Australian censors banned importation. Authorities treated the manual as a practical danger rather than merely offensive literature. This is one of the clearest examples where a book ban focused on operational harm.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.author: Anti-Defamation League

counterReadings.title: Denying the Holocaust

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
